name: Las Presillas Green Route 4.2
name_original: Parque de las Presillas – running circular desde Alcorcón por el cauce del Arroyo Butarque
description: Enjoy an easy 2.8-mile (4.5 km) jogging route on the Las Presillas Green Route 4.2 in Parque de Las Presillas, Alcorcón.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Alcorcón are characterized by a significant number of green areas and urban parks, providing ample space for outdoor activities. The city, located southwest of Madrid, features a transitional geological zone with diverse terrain, including deposits of sands and clays. Key natural features include the Arroyo Butarque, which flows through Parque de Las Presillas, offering a picturesque setting for running. With over 340 hectares of green spaces, Alcorcón provides a healthy urban environment for runners. Best jogging routes around Alcorcón The most popular jogging route is Las Presillas Green Route 4.2, a 2.8 miles (4.5 km) trail that takes around 30 minutes to complete. This easy route is primarily on paved surfaces within Parque de Las Presillas. Another top favourite among local runners is Polvoranca Park Entrance – Polvoranca Lake loop from Puerta del Sur, a moderate 6.8 miles (11 km) path. This route, mostly on paved surfaces, features the notable María Pascuala Lake within Polvoranca Park. Local runners also love the Buero loop from Alcorcón Central, a 7.8 miles (12.6 km) trail leading through urban green areas, often completed in about 1 hour 20 minutes. Jogging around Alcorcón is defined by extensive green areas, urban parks, and the Arroyo Butarque, offering a mix of paved and natural surfaces. The network provides options for various ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging moderate runs. The routes in Alcorcón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from several reviews. More than 1,100 runners have used komoot to explore Alcorcón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Alcorcón?
A: Alcorcón offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 75 trails available for you to explore. These routes cater to various fitness levels, from easy jogs to more challenging runs.

Q: What makes Alcorcón a good place for jogging?
A: Alcorcón is an excellent destination for jogging due to its extensive green areas and urban parks, totaling over 340 hectares. The city features diverse terrain, including the picturesque Arroyo Butarque, and actively promotes an active lifestyle with facilities like 'Street Workout' areas and a municipal running club.

Q: What do other runners appreciate most about the trails in Alcorcón?
A: The running trails in Alcorcón are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars. Over 1,100 runners have used komoot to explore the area, often praising the variety of routes through extensive green spaces and urban parks, offering both paved and natural surfaces.

Q: Are there easy jogging routes suitable for beginners in Alcorcón?
A: Yes, Alcorcón has several easy jogging routes perfect for beginners. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e957989121/las-presillas-green-route-4-2" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Presillas Green Route 4.2&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 4.5 km (2.8 miles) trail primarily on paved surfaces within Parque de Las Presillas.

Q: Can I find more challenging running trails in Alcorcón?
A: Absolutely. While Alcorcón offers many moderate routes, for those seeking a challenge, the city's location provides access to other natural areas within the wider Madrid region, such as the Sierra de Guadarrama, for more demanding trail running. Within Alcorcón, you'll find over 60 moderate routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31604535" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buero loop from Alcorcón Central&lt;/a&gt;, which is 12.6 km (7.8 miles) long.

Q: Are there circular running routes available in Alcorcón?
A: Yes, many of Alcorcón's running routes are circular, offering convenient loops for your run. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23850422" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Polvoranca Park Entrance – Polvoranca Lake loop from Puerta del Sur&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 11 km (6.8 miles) circular path that takes you around María Pascuala Lake.

Q: Which parks in Alcorcón are best for a run?
A: Several parks in Alcorcón are ideal for running. &lt;strong&gt;Parque de Las Presillas&lt;/strong&gt; is home to the scenic Arroyo Butarque. &lt;strong&gt;Polvoranca Park&lt;/strong&gt; features the notable María Pascuala Lake. Other popular options include &lt;strong&gt;Parque del Buero&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Los Castillos&lt;/strong&gt;, which offers an interesting backdrop of monumental sculptures.

Q: Are there any scenic features or landmarks to see while running in Alcorcón?
A: Yes, Alcorcón's routes offer several points of interest. You can enjoy the picturesque setting of the &lt;strong&gt;Arroyo Butarque&lt;/strong&gt; flowing through Parque de Las Presillas, or run past the beautiful &lt;strong&gt;María Pascuala Lake&lt;/strong&gt; in Polvoranca Park. The urban park of Los Castillos also features a collection of monumental sculptures. For historical sights nearby, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3816969" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Royal Palace of Madrid&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1499151" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Toledo Bridge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Alcorcón?
A: While specific regulations for each park may vary, Alcorcón's extensive green areas and urban parks generally welcome dogs, often requiring them to be on a leash. It's always a good idea to check local signage within specific parks like Parque de Las Presillas or Polvoranca Park for any particular rules regarding pets.

Q: Are the running routes in Alcorcón suitable for families?
A: Many of Alcorcón's running routes, especially the easier and paved ones within its numerous parks, are suitable for families. The city's commitment to green spaces and recreational areas makes it a great place for families to enjoy outdoor activities together. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e957989121/las-presillas-green-route-4-2" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Presillas Green Route 4.2&lt;/a&gt; is an easy option for all fitness levels.

Q: Is public transport available to access jogging routes in Alcorcón?
A: Yes, Alcorcón is well-connected by public transport, making many jogging routes easily accessible. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31604535" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buero loop from Alcorcón Central&lt;/a&gt; start near major transport hubs, allowing you to reach the trails conveniently without a car.

Q: Are there parking facilities near the running trails in Alcorcón?
A: Given Alcorcón's urban environment and numerous parks, you can generally find parking facilities near the popular running areas. Parks like Polvoranca Park are easily accessible, and routes often start from locations with available parking, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6925983" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buero loop from ALDI&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: Las Presillas Park offers a beautiful escape to connect with nature. Very close to the urban center of Alcorcón, inside the park you can find tables and benches, trails of easy difficulty, one of them that runs along the bed of the Butarque stream. A very picturesque place for a quiet lap.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog Las Presillas Green Route 4.2?
faqs.answer: Based on its length of approximately 4.5 km and easy difficulty, most joggers can complete this route in about 30-35 minutes.

faqs.question: Is Las Presillas Green Route 4.2 suitable for beginner joggers or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is rated as "easy" and has a relatively flat profile with only about 43 meters of elevation gain, making it ideal for beginners and families looking for a pleasant jogging experience.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this jogging route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily follows the Parque de las Presillas Trail, which is likely compacted earth or a similar accessible surface, typical of greenways. It's generally smooth and well-maintained, suitable for jogging.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Las Presillas Green Route 4.2 trailhead?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for "Las Presillas Green Route 4.2" are not widely documented, as it is located within Parque de las Presillas in Alcorcón, you should look for parking facilities near the park entrances. Public transport options to the park may also be available.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Las Presillas Green Route 4.2?
faqs.answer: Regulations for dogs can vary within parks. While many green routes are dog-friendly, it's best to check local signage at Parque de las Presillas upon arrival. Typically, dogs are permitted but must be kept on a leash.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for Las Presillas Green Route 4.2 or Parque de las Presillas?
faqs.answer: Generally, public green routes and parks like Parque de las Presillas do not require permits or entrance fees for access. However, it's always a good idea to check for any specific local regulations or temporary notices at the park entrance.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: Given its location, spring and autumn offer the most pleasant temperatures for jogging, avoiding the heat of summer and potential chill or wet conditions of winter. However, the route is accessible year-round, with appropriate clothing for the season.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route covers a significant portion of the "Ruta Verde 4.2 Las Presillas" (84%) and also includes sections of the "Ruta Verde 4.1 Parque Lineal de Butarque" (71%). You might also briefly encounter parts of the "Camino de la Canaleja" and "Vereda de Castilla" trails.

faqs.question: Are there any viewpoints or natural features along the way?
faqs.answer: The route is situated along the Butarque stream bed within Parque de las Presillas, offering natural scenery. While specific named viewpoints are not highlighted, you can expect pleasant views of the park's green spaces and the stream.

faqs.question: Is Las Presillas Green Route 4.2 a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the "Las Presillas Green Route 4.2" is designed as a circular jogging route, allowing you to start and finish at the same point without retracing your steps.

faqs.question: Are there cafes or facilities near the trail?
faqs.answer: As the route is located within Parque de las Presillas, it's common for urban parks to have nearby facilities. While specific cafes are not listed, you can likely find amenities in the surrounding Alcorcón area, or within the park itself.
